---
created: "2026-03-22T00:00:00Z"
id: task-sv0vm-milestone-2
key: sv0vm-milestone-2
state: draft
tags:
    - sv0
    - sv0vm
    - milestone-2
    - bytecode
title: "milestone 2: sv0vm bytecode virtual machine (SML/NJ)"
type: task
---

# milestone 2: sv0vm bytecode virtual machine (SML/NJ)

## purpose

build the sv0 bytecode virtual machine in SML/NJ. when milestone 2 is
complete, sv0c can compile sv0 source to bytecode, and sv0vm can execute
that bytecode. this provides the fastest iteration cycle during development
(no external C compiler needed) and powers the REPL.

milestone 2 is complete when:
- the bytecode format is defined and documented in sv0doc
- sv0c has a VM backend that emits bytecode
- sv0vm loads and executes bytecode files
- contract checks work at runtime in the VM
- the REPL can evaluate expressions interactively
- sv0vm passes the same test suite as the C backend

## dependencies

/require: sml
/require: bash

## configuration

/env: SV0C_ROOT=sv0c
/env: SV0VM_ROOT=sv0vm
/env: SV0DOC_ROOT=sv0doc
/env: BUILD_DIR=sv0vm/build

## prerequisite: bootstrap compiler

the VM backend is an additional backend for sv0c. the frontend (lexer through
contract analyzer) and IR must be complete before the VM backend can emit
bytecode.

/assert: test -f $SV0C_ROOT/src/ir/ir.sml
/assert: test -f $SV0C_ROOT/src/ir/lowering.sml

## phase 1: bytecode format design

/include: task/sv0vm-bytecode-format.Rmd

/capture: BYTECODE_STATUS
/assert: test "$BYTECODE_STATUS" != "FAIL"

## phase 2: interpreter core

/include: task/sv0vm-interpreter.Rmd

/capture: INTERPRETER_STATUS
/assert: test "$INTERPRETER_STATUS" != "FAIL"

## phase 3: runtime support

/include: task/sv0vm-runtime.Rmd

/capture: RUNTIME_STATUS
/assert: test "$RUNTIME_STATUS" != "FAIL"

## phase 4: VM backend for sv0c

/include: task/sv0vm-vm-backend.Rmd

/capture: VMBACKEND_STATUS
/assert: test "$VMBACKEND_STATUS" != "FAIL"

## phase 5: integration testing

/ai: design an integration test suite for sv0vm that mirrors the C backend
     tests. every program that compiles and runs via the C backend should
     produce identical output when compiled to bytecode and run on sv0vm.

the pipeline is wired from the toolchain root: `make integration-vm`, `./scripts/sv0 integration-vm`, or `make -C sv0c integration-vm` (compiles selected `.sv0` with `sv0c --target=vm`, runs `.sv0b` via `sv0vm/scripts/run_sv0b.sml`). SML-only CI: `make ci` or `./scripts/sv0 ci`. to include **sv0-mcp** tests as well, use `make ci-all` or `./scripts/sv0 ci-all` (requires `uv`).

/run: 01-verify-setup.sh
/run: 02-integration-test.sh

## milestone completion check

/assert: test -f $SV0VM_ROOT/src/bytecode/bytecode.sml
/assert: test -f $SV0VM_ROOT/src/interpreter/interpreter.sml
/assert: test -f $SV0VM_ROOT/src/runtime/runtime.sml

/ai: summarize the state of milestone 2. report:
     - bytecode format completeness
     - which sv0 features the VM supports
     - performance characteristics (vs C backend)
     - REPL status
     - readiness for milestone 3 (self-hosting)
